paper_title: Pollen Analysis and Data Processing in Honey Samples

methods.description: This method involves the preparation and analysis of honey samples to identify and count pollen grains and honeydew elements. It is a semiquantitative approach modified from a quantitative method to provide a reliable estimate of the pollen spectrum in honey samples.

steps.description: The honey sample was dissolved in water, and the solid particles were centrifuged for the microscopical analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: dissolve honey sample in water and centrifuge for analysis

steps.description: From the original honey sample a 10 g subsample was taken and dissolved in 20 ml of distilled water, and centrifuged for 10 min at 3 500 r/min, after which the supernatant liquid was drawn off.
steps.searchable_summary: centrifuge honey subsample and remove supernatant

steps.description: A further 10 ml of water was added to the sediment and centrifuged 5 min at 3 500 r/min, after which the liquid was drawn off.
steps.searchable_summary: add water to sediment and centrifuge again

steps.description: The sediment was transferred with a capillary pipette to a microscope slide, and dispersed evenly over an area of 20 mm X 20 mm.
The slide was dried for 24 h at 30 °C. The mounting medium was glycerine jelly.
steps.searchable_summary: dry slide and mount with glycerine jelly

steps.description: On each slide, all the pollen grains and honeydew elements were counted and identified on a 20mm line across the 20 mm X 20 mm square.
steps.searchable_summary: count and identify pollen grains on slide

steps.description: The accuracy of the counts was evaluated by comparing the results of the parallel counts in a sample of 14 pairs of slides.
steps.searchable_summary: validate pollen count accuracy with parallel slide comparison
inputs: pollen count data from parallel slides

methods.description: This method involves the statistical analysis of pollen spectra data from honey samples collected over two seasons, considering factors such as intercorrelations between pollen types, annual variation, regional variation, and variation connected with bee race.

steps.description: The variation in the pollen spectra was analysed separately for the two seasons.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze pollen spectra variation for two seasons

steps.description: To study the annual variation, only those apiaries were included from which one or more honey samples were received in both 1977 and 1978.
steps.searchable_summary: filter apiaries with samples from both years for annual variation study

steps.description: The regional division adopted in studying the regional variation is based on the length of the thermal growing season.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze regional variation based on thermal growing season

steps.description: In the statistical analysis, three groups of bees were distinguished: Italian bees, dark bees, and bees of mixed race.
steps.searchable_summary: distinguish bee groups for statistical analysis

steps.description: The statistical analyses were done by the H-programmes of the Computing Centre, University of Helsinki.
